title: Load Salesforce Object Field Details on Drill-In
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to click on a Salesforce object in the scorecard view and drill into a detail page showing field-level information, so that I can review and annotate individual fields for that object.
description:
When a user clicks on a Salesforce object from the scorecard view, the application opens a new page that displays field-level details for that object. The data is loaded by integrating with the Salesforce REST API describe method.

The detail page presents a data table combining Salesforce field metadata (API name, type, length, etc.) with user-editable input fields for documentation and mapping purposes.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking a Salesforce object in the scorecard navigates to a detail page
Detail page loads field-level metadata from Salesforce via the REST API describe endpoint
Data table displays both Salesforce metadata columns and user input columns
Page loads the correct object based on the URL parameter
ux_changes: New detail page opens when a user clicks an object in the scorecard. Displays a data table with Salesforce field metadata alongside editable user fields.
middleware_changes: Server-side integration with the Salesforce REST API describe endpoint to retrieve field-level metadata for the selected object.
api_changes: Consumes the Salesforce REST API describe method to load object field details. Endpoint returns field names, types, lengths, and other metadata.
implementation_notes: Integration uses the SFDC REST API describe method documented in the Sprint 5 Epic. The data table mixes API-sourced metadata with locally stored user annotations.
